closed_children.title: Greenfield skeleton: alongside module tree + op-dispatch entrypoint (§0/§8)
Greenfield skeleton (in-place): new crate root + §8 dispatch, old src deleted
§1/§2 XDG layout + single balls orphan branch + symlink registry
Vocab: rename anchor→landing in landed doc-comments [bl-2e26]

description: Build the greenfield architecture in bl-2e26 (§0–§17) as a fresh implementation that REPLACES current balls IN PLACE at the crate root — same `balls` crate / `bl` binary, a major version bump at cutover. NOT a separate crate, NOT a coexistence/next:: namespace, NOT a rebrand. The system-installed `bl` is the self-hoster and keeps tracking this work, so the repo's `main` need NOT build a functional `bl` during the rewrite (it carries a stub/partial `bl` until cutover). Rules: do NOT `make install` from this tree until cutover, and never point a new `bl` at this repo's task list. Tests bootstrap a throwaway /tmp dir and exec the freshly-built binary by path. Strategy (confirmed 2026-06-05): finish development, THEN run the §17 migration on THIS repo + install + cut over as the final validation story. bl-f021 (XDG relocation) is CLOSED — subsumed here. Source of truth = bl-2e26; this epic implements, it does not re-litigate design. Subtasks are --dep-linked (claim-blockers) so independent layers parallelize. P0 skeleton DELIVERED: bl-505f first shipped a src/next/ quarantine (90faf06) under a wrong "alongside" model; bl-5c65 superseded it (f53259b) — deleted the ~140 legacy modules + old tests/, greenfield now lives at the crate root (src/lib.rs+main.rs+verb.rs+op.rs, the §0/§8 spine). The L0 phases' deps name the closed bl-505f, which resolves them (substrate exists).
title: Implement greenfield balls (per spec bl-2e26)
